meta.title: Nando — Andrej Kristancic, Goriška Brda
meta.description: Natural wines from 5.5 hectares of ponca soil, where Collio and Brda share a border and a terroir.

hero.text: Andrej Kristancic makes wine on 5.5 hectares that sit across two countries. He doesn't always notice when he crosses the border.

place.text: The border between Italian Collio and Slovenian Brda runs through vineyards and unpaved roads. The soil on both sides is the same: ponca — layers of marl and sandstone that crumble in your hands. The Adriatic pushes warm air from the south-west, the Julian Alps send cold drafts down. This is where Ribolla Gialla, Malvasia and Friulano grow.

method.text: Organic farming. Spontaneous fermentation. Maceration on the skins — how long depends on the grape and the year. No aggressive filtration. The wine carries the mineral signature of the ponca it grew in.
